messages.content:
Prove the following Lean 4 theorem. Edit the file at `testbed/Main.lean` in place: replace the `sorry` with the complete proof. The file already contains the theorem statement; do not rewrite the statement.

Current contents of `testbed/Main.lean`:
```lean
-- Synthetic problem added by lea/eval/run_minif2f_parallel smoke test.
-- Safe to delete; not part of the real miniF2F set.
import Mathlib

theorem lea_smoke_trivial : 1 + 1 = 2 := by
  sorry

```

Use the `lean_check` tool on `testbed/Main.lean` to verify your work. The proof is complete when `lean_check` returns OK with no errors and no warnings, the file contains no `sorry`, and no banned term (exact?, apply?, simp?, decide?, native_decide, axiom ).

Use `search_mathlib` to find relevant Mathlib lemmas. You may edit files anywhere inside the Lake project; your edits will be captured as part of the result.

content.text: The proof is complete. `rfl` works directly because `1 + 1 = 2` is true by definition. The file has been successfully verified with no errors or warnings.
